summary: Phase 53 Digital Twin backend testing — Blender headless conversion pipeline + Trimble Connect embed URL. All 19 pytest tests PASSED (100%). Verified OBJ→GLB conversion completes <10s producing valid GLB file (glTF magic bytes), retry endpoint works, multi-format upload (.dae/.fbx/.stl/.ply/.obj) all routed to Blender engine, .skp routed to cloudconvert path, .glb direct upload no-converts, Trimble URL validation (rejects non-trimble.com), permission gates (specialist/client → 403 on /trimble, client without DT pro → 403 on upload).
critical_code_review_comments: digital_twin.py is 2330 lines — exceeds 700 line guideline; consider splitting into sub-routers (uploads, conversions, projects, operator) for maintainability.

Trimble URL validator (line 2217) accepts ANY URL containing 'trimble.com' or 'sketchup.com' as substring — a domain like 'https://malicious-trimble.com.evil.io/' would pass. Recommend parsing URL and checking host ends with '.trimble.com' or '.sketchup.com'.
Auth: login response uses httpOnly cookie only (no token in JSON body). This is fine for browsers but worth noting in API docs for programmatic clients.
test_credentials: admin@propmanage.io/Admin123!, operator@propmanage.io/Op123!, specialist@propmanage.io/Spec123!, client@propmanage.io/Client123! — all working via cookie session auth.
seed_data_creation: Module-scoped fixture creates one project 'TEST_p53_blender_proj' and cleans up via DELETE in teardown. Each test uploads its own tiny OBJ payload (185 bytes).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth uses requests.Session() (cookie-based); login response body has NO token field — only sets cookies. Use session.cookies + session.headers when calling endpoints. Blender 3.4.1 conversion of tiny OBJ completes in ~3s. Operator user has trimble-PATCH access; specialist/client get 403. Client lacks digital_twin_pro so upload to admin's project returns 403/404. .skp route returns conversion_engine='cloudconvert' (kept for future). .glb direct upload sets kind='model' and no conversion fields.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures encountered. Initial test runs failed only due to (a) missing REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env at pytest invocation (fixed by passing inline) and (b) initial test assumed Bearer-token auth but backend uses cookie-session auth (fixed by switching to requests.Session).
